m_mw_01_validation.false_positive_profile: Unchanged from previous baseline (32-byte hash without 0x still matches). The greedy {64,} can extend a match further than necessary, but since the entire run is replaced by REDACTED, no partial-secret leak is possible. Two adjacent 64-hex secrets are now both redacted in one shot (previously: both passed).

regex_engineering_review.lookbehind_lookahead_correctness: Both anchors are negated character classes â€” atomic, no backtracking surface. The class `[0-9a-fA-F]` matches the EXACT set used for hex chars elsewhere in the module, so lookarounds and the body class are consistent.
regex_engineering_review.redos_assessment: No ReDoS surface. The body is a fixed character class with a `{64,}` quantifier; no nested alternation, no overlap. Worst-case work is O(N) on a length-N input. Confirmed by inspection â€” no `(a+)+`, no `(a|a)+`, no catastrophic-backtracking shape.
regex_engineering_review.ordering_with_prefixed_patterns: scrubString chains: HEX_130_WITH_PREFIX_RE â†’ HEX_64_WITH_PREFIX_RE â†’ HEX_BARE_RUN_RE. For prefixed inputs (`0x` + N hex), the prefixed regex matches first and replaces with literal `<redacted:relayer-key>` â€” no longer hex, so the bare-run pass cannot re-trigger. For bare runs, HEX_BARE_RUN_RE catches them. The chain is order-correct.
regex_engineering_review.case_flag_removal: Removal of the `i` flag is benign because the character class `[0-9a-fA-F]` is already explicit. Mixed-case `'AbCd'.repeat(32) + 'aA'` test confirms.
regex_engineering_review.consolidation_decision: Audit-fixer collapsed bare-64 and bare-130 patterns into a single `{64,}` quantifier. Acceptable: any maximal hex run of length â‰Ą 64 at non-hex boundaries is suspicious; the single-pattern form is simpler and provably covers both shapes. The audit-fixer's note ('say so and I'll split') â€” I do NOT recommend splitting; the consolidated form is preferable for maintainability and is functionally complete.

fuzz_test_coverage_review.coverage_gap_check: All four scenarios from my round-1 audit finding are explicitly tested. No additional scenarios required.

T12-01_errors_module_deletion.evidence: errors.ts was never re-exported from index.ts â€” confirmed by `grep -n 'errors' packages/middleware/src/index.ts` returning empty. No imports from `./errors.js` anywhere in src/, scripts/, or tests (verified with grep). The two error classes that errors.ts re-exported (`MalformedPaymentHeaderError`, `NetworkMismatchError`) are still defined in x402.ts and settle.ts respectively, and all consumers import them directly from those origin modules. Test suite count drops by 49 (errors.test.ts â†’ 49 tests) and that matches the observed 253â†’208 delta exactly. No security regression.
T12-01_errors_module_deletion.anchor: D1 (canonical reason strings) â€” verified: x402.ts:33+ still emits canonical reason strings; core.ts:build402 produces the wire body and uses the canonical reasons.

T12-02_SettlementFailedReason_union.evidence: Pure type-level documentation. Adds a new exported type `SettlementFailedReason = SettleReason | 'chain_id_mismatch'` that documents the wire-observable superset. No runtime change. No new public API surface (not re-exported from index.ts â€” verified).

T12-03_internal_error_rename.evidence: Replaces wire reason `internal_error` (not in any taxonomy) with `chain_id_mismatch` (D18 event name). Both call sites: (a) unknown-network at request entry, (b) NetworkMismatchError catch from settle. The corresponding chain_id_mismatch event was already emitted at both sites â€” only the body.reason string changes. No information disclosure change (still a typed token; no echo of attacker input). core.test.ts:567-580 updated consistently. No SecurityLogger payload shape change.

T12-04_relayer_key_docstring.evidence: Rewrites the doc to accurately describe the WeakMap implementation instead of the spec-original `#key` claim. The new doc explicitly notes the WeakMap is strictly stronger than `#private`. Useful audit-trail clarity; no code change.

T12-05_PaymentVaultImpl_locals_rename.evidence: Pure local-variable rename: `usdc` â†’ `usdcToken`, `feeBps` â†’ `currentFeeBps`. Eliminates shadowing of the `IPaymentSplitterFactory.usdc()` and `IPaymentSplitterFactory.feeBps()` interface methods (style improvement). Control flow identical: balance read, fee math, developer transfer first, treasury transfer second, event emission. `nonReentrant` modifier preserved. CEI ordering preserved. Slither re-run shows IDENTICAL 4 findings as the pre-fix baseline â€” no new detector triggered. test_Withdraw_HappyPathDeveloperFirst, test_Withdraw_ReentrancyBlocked_ViaMaliciousTreasury, test_Withdraw_FeeBps0_NoSecondTransfer, test_Withdraw_FeeBps1000_Splits90_10, test_Withdraw_DustGrossTruncatesFee, test_Withdraw_UsesCurrentFeeBpsAtWithdrawTime, test_Withdraw_Boundary199, test_Withdraw_Boundary200 all PASS after the rename. forge_test counts unchanged at 52/52.

informational_finding.title: Greedy {64,} quantifier can extend a match arbitrarily far when an upstream maintainer accidentally introduces a very long bare-hex payload
informational_finding.description: The `[0-9a-fA-F]{64,}` quantifier is unbounded on the upper side. For pathological inputs (e.g. a malicious operator pasting an entire 1MB bare-hex blob into a log line), the redactor will replace the entire blob with a single REDACTED token. This is the correct security behaviour but produces a log entry that destroys diagnostic context around the blob. This is NOT a security defect â€” the redaction succeeds â€” but a minor operational observation.
informational_finding.recommendation: No code change required. Optionally cap the quantifier at a generous upper bound (e.g. `{64,4096}`) so a runaway hex blob doesn't single-handedly replace gigabytes of log context. Not blocking; this is an operational ergonomics note, not a security gap.

signoff.rationale: Round-1 M-MW-01 is resolved with a defensible regex implementation, four explicit fuzz tests, and no test regression. Spillover changes (T12 module deletion + rename, T14 comments) introduce no security regressions: errors.ts was unused dead code, the wire-reason rename is a taxonomy consolidation with consistent test updates, the contract local-variable rename preserves CEI + reentrancy ordering and slither/forge agree. All deferred low/informational findings from my round-1 audit are accepted as post-Final-Wave items per the audit-fixer's scope bound. Recommend audit-fix is CLEAR; no round-2 needed from the security side.
